title: Useful and Useless: Transition-Opening Usefulness and Closure-Producing Efficiency
core_claim: Useful is not mere efficiency; useless is not mere inefficiency.

definitions.useful: Transition-opening capacity: opens questions, reveals hidden costs, preserves re-entry, creates new judgment axes, and enables redesign.
definitions.useless: Closure-producing efficiency: appears efficient, valid, practical, orthodox, or useful while closing questions, hiding costs, and blocking re-entry.

cases: Song Si-yeol / Samu-nanjeok as closure-producing orthodoxy
Generative AI and the destabilization of the human-only creativity default
warnings: Do not equate high cost with usefulness.
Do not equate efficiency with uselessness.
Do not use useful/useless as moral praise or blame.
Do not demonize historical individuals when the relevant target is structural closure.
